title: ranunculus magnitorifer
side_effects: Ranunculus magnitorifer can have side effects if used improperly, such as inducing severe skin irritation, allergic reactions, and gastrointestinal issues in some individuals. Prolonged exposure to the plant's sap has been known to cause blisters, rashes, and in severe cases, permanent scarring. Ingesting even small amounts of the plant's toxic compounds can lead to nausea, vomiting, and diarrhea. Improper handling or consumption of Ranunculus magnitorifer can also trigger more severe reactions, including anaphylaxis and organ failure in extreme cases.
